info.title: Hologram API
info.description: REST API for Hologram — a collaborative worldbuilding and roleplay system built on an entity-facts model. Entities represent characters, locations, items, or any concept; facts are freeform strings that define their behaviour and appearance.

tags.name: entities
tags.description: Entity CRUD — the core object in Hologram

tags.name: facts
tags.description: Freeform fact strings attached to an entity

tags.name: templates
tags.description: Nunjucks templates that override the default system prompt

tags.name: memories
tags.description: Long-term memories recalled via semantic similarity

tags.name: discord-channels
tags.description: Read-only mirror of Discord channel state

tags.name: debug
tags.description: Introspection endpoints — binding graph, eval errors, embedding coverage, trace

get.description: Returns a paginated list of entities, optionally filtered by name substring.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results. Capped at 500.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

post.description: Creates a new entity. Names do not have to be unique.

get.description: Returns a single entity together with its full fact list.

delete.description: Deletes the entity and all associated facts, memories, and bindings.

get.description: Returns all facts for an entity in insertion order.

post.description: Appends a new fact to the entity. Facts are freeform strings; they may include `$if` conditions, `$respond` directives, and `{{macro}}` references.

get.description: Returns all configuration columns for the entity. Most values are JSON-encoded strings stored in SQLite text columns — null means the system default applies.

patch.description: Partially updates entity configuration. Only the supplied fields are written; omitted fields are left unchanged.

get.description: Returns the entity's Nunjucks template that controls system-prompt formatting. `null` means the built-in DEFAULT_TEMPLATE is used.

put.description: Replaces the entity's main template. Set to `null` to revert to the built-in default. The template is rendered with Nunjucks; use `{% call send_as(role) %}` blocks to designate message roles.

get.description: Returns the entity's system-parameter template. This is rendered and passed as the AI SDK `system` field — a separate channel from the main message list. `null` means an empty system parameter.
Returns all long-term memories for the entity. Memories are retrieved at inference time via semantic similarity against recent messages.
Returns all web chat channels. Web channels are isolated from Discord; they have their own message history and entity roster.

post.description: Creates a new web channel with the given entity roster. The channel ID is auto-generated as `web:<uuid>`.

patch.description: Updates the channel name and/or entity roster. Only supplied fields are changed.

delete.description: Deletes the channel and its message history.

get.description: Returns recent messages for a channel in chronological order.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of messages to return. Capped at 200.

post.description: Stores a user message, broadcasts it to SSE subscribers, then asynchronously triggers entity responses. AI responses arrive via the SSE stream, not in this response.

201.description: Message stored and AI response triggered.

get.description:
Opens a Server-Sent Events stream for the channel. Events are newline-delimited JSON objects prefixed with `data: `. The connection stays open until the client disconnects.

Event types:
- `message` — a new message was stored (`{ type: "message", message: ApiMessage }`)
- `forget` — the context window was reset (`{ type: "forget", forget_at: string }`)
- AI streaming events (token chunks, tool calls, etc.)

post.description: Instructs entities to ignore messages before the current timestamp. Useful for starting a fresh scene without deleting history. The cut-off time is broadcast to SSE subscribers.
Fires entity responses for the channel without a user message. Useful for prompting a continuation or resuming a paused scene.

get.description: Returns all Discord channels that have at least one entity binding, plus DM channels, sorted by most recent message descending. Requires the Discord bot to be running.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of messages to return. Capped at 200.

post.summary: Send a message to a Discord channel
post.description: Sends a message via the Discord bot. For server channels the message is sent through a webhook so it can carry a custom name and avatar. DMs are sent directly. Returns 503 if the bot is not connected.

get.summary: Subscribe to Discord channel SSE stream
get.description: Same SSE protocol as `/api/channels/{id}/stream`, but for a Discord channel.
Returns the full Discord→entity binding table, optionally scoped to a specific guild or channel.
Returns deduplicated fact evaluation errors. Each row represents a unique (entity, condition, error) triple — duplicate errors are suppressed until the error changes.
Returns aggregate counts of embedded vs total facts and memories. Useful for checking whether the background embedding job is keeping up.
Returns embedding coverage details for a specific entity's facts and memories.
Evaluates all facts for the entity and returns a trace showing which facts were included, which were conditional, and why each was included or excluded. Useful for debugging `$if` conditions.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Channel ID to use as evaluation context. Defaults to `web:debug`.

parameters.name: guild
parameters.description: Discord guild ID for scope resolution.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: Channel ID (web or Discord snowflake) to simulate.

get.description: Runs the respond-decision logic for all entities bound to the channel and reports whether each would respond and why. No message is sent.

parameters.name: guild
parameters.description: Discord guild ID for scope resolution.

get.description: Returns this OpenAPI 3.1 specification as JSON.

discordChannelId.name: id
discordChannelId.description: Discord channel ID (snowflake, 17–19 digit integer string).

BadRequest.description: Bad request — invalid JSON, missing required field, or invalid ID.

ApiEntity.description: An entity — the core object in Hologram. Represents any concept: character, location, item, help topic, etc.

owned_by.description: Discord user ID of the owner, or null.

ApiFact.description: A freeform fact string attached to an entity. Facts are the primary input to the AI pipeline. They may include `$if` conditions, `$respond` directives, and `{{macro}}` references.

content.description: The fact text. Examples: `is a character`, `$if mentioned: $respond`, `has silver hair`, `is in {{entity:12}}`.

ApiEntityConfig.description: Per-entity configuration. All fields are nullable — null means inherit the system default.

config_context.description: A `$if`-style boolean expression controlling which messages this entity sees. Example: `channel.id == '1234567890'`.

config_model.description: LLM override in `provider:model` format. Example: `google:gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06`. Null uses the server default.

config_respond.description: Boolean expression that overrides whether this entity responds. Evaluated after fact-level `$if`/`$respond` conditions.

config_stream_delimiters.description: JSON array of delimiter strings used to split streamed output into separate messages.

config_avatar.description: URL of the entity's avatar image, used in Discord webhook messages.

config_memory.description: Memory retrieval mode. Controls whether memories are retrieved for this entity.

config_freeform.description: Whether the entity has free-form tool access (1) or restricted (0).

config_strip.description: JSON array of regex patterns. Matches are stripped from the entity's output before it is sent.

config_view.description: JSON-encoded permission list for who can view this entity. `"@everyone"` (as JSON string) means no restriction.

config_edit.description: JSON-encoded permission list for who can edit this entity.

config_use.description: JSON-encoded permission list for who can use (bind) this entity.

config_blacklist.description: JSON-encoded list of user/role IDs blocked from interacting with this entity. Null means no blacklist.

config_thinking.description: Extended thinking budget in tokens, or null to disable. Only supported by models with thinking capability.

config_collapse.description: Message collapse strategy for long histories.

config_keywords.description: JSON array of keyword strings. When any keyword appears in a message, the entity is considered mentioned.

ApiMemory.description: A long-term memory for an entity. Retrieved at inference time via semantic similarity against recent messages.

source_message_id.description: Discord message ID where this memory originated, if any.

source_channel_id.description: Discord channel ID where this memory originated, if any.

source_guild_id.description: Discord guild ID where this memory originated, if any.

frecency.description: Frecency score (frequency × recency). Higher = more likely to be surfaced.

ApiWebChannel.description: A web chat channel. Web channels are independent of Discord and have their own message history.

entity_ids.description: IDs of entities active in this channel.

ApiDiscordChannel.description: A Discord channel or DM that has at least one entity binding.

name.description: Channel name, or null for DMs.

ApiMessage.description: A stored message from either a user or an entity.

discord_message_id.description: Discord message snowflake, if this message originated on Discord.

data.description: JSON-encoded rich Discord data (embeds, components, attachments). Null for web-only messages.

discord_type.description: Binding scope: `channel` = responds in that channel, `guild` = responds in all channels of that server, `user` = persona binding.

condition.description: The `$if` expression that failed, if applicable.

model.description: Embedding model in use, or null if embeddings are disabled.

ApiFactTrace.description: Evaluation trace for a single fact.

raw.description: The raw fact string as stored in the database.

conditional.description: Whether the fact has a `$if` condition.

result.description: The evaluated result of the condition. Null if evaluation errored.

category.description: Fact category: `always` = unconditional fact, `conditional` = has `$if`, `directive` = `$respond` or similar.

included.description: Whether this fact was included in the final prompt.

respond_source.description: Which fact or config field triggered the respond decision.

owned_by.description: Discord user ID of the owner.

entity_ids.description: Entity IDs to include in the channel.

name.description: New display name, or null to clear.

author_id.description: Caller's user identifier. Defaults to a generated ID.

author_name.description: Display name shown in history. Defaults to `"user"`.

entity_id.description: Entity whose name and avatar are used for the webhook message.

author_name.description: Fallback display name if `entity_id` is not provided.

is_dm.description: Set to true to send a direct message instead of a webhook message.

template.description: Nunjucks template source. Null resets to the built-in DEFAULT_TEMPLATE.

system_template.description: Nunjucks template rendered into the AI SDK `system` field. Null means an empty system parameter.

ai_response.description: Always null — AI responses arrive via SSE.

forget_at.description: Messages before this timestamp will be excluded from AI context.
